perturbation.original_text: 1.17 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means: (a) with respect to Sanofi, [***], consistent with [***] that [***], taking into account [***], including [***] and (b) with respect to RevMed, [***], consistent with [***] that [***], taking into account [***], including [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.17 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means: (a) with respect to Sanofi, to use efforts in line with industry standard. (b) with respect to RevMed, to use efforts in line with industry standard.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation alters the definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' by omitting the specific, detailed criteria for both Sanofi and RevMed. Instead, it replaces the nuanced language with a general statement of industry-standard efforts. This introduces an in-text contradiction because various sections of the contract rely on the original, more precise definition to determine compliance and allocate responsibilities. The sections that refer to 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' will now be ambiguous, potentially creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.7 Diligence. Consistent with [***] or as otherwise agreed by the Parties, Sanofi shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts [***] to file and seek approval for an MAA for at least one Product in all of such countries or, in the case of the Major Market Countries in the European Union, through the centralized European Union approval process. If Sanofi materially breaches its obligation set forth in this Section 5.7, [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the detailed definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' with a general 'industry standard' definition. The contradicted text in Section 5.7 relies on the specific, nuanced obligations initially outlined in the original definition to determine whether Sanofi has breached its diligence obligation. The removal of these specific obligations creates ambiguity and makes it unclear how to assess whether Sanofi's efforts meet the 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' standard, directly impacting the enforceability of Section 5.7.

perturbation.original_text: 1.38 "FTE" means a full time equivalent person year (consisting of [***] hours per year) of work as an employee or contractor [***] hereunder as tracked by each Party using its respective standard practice and methodologies. For clarity, [***] will not constitute FTEs. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the time of a single individual will not account for more than one FTE for a given Calendar Year (or applicable pro-rata portion of an FTE during any Calendar Quarter or other period of less than a Calendar Year).
perturbation.changed_text: 1.38 "FTE" means a unit to measure the efforts being done in this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific number of hours that constitute an FTE and removing the clarification about what doesn't count as an FTE, this change introduces an internal contradiction. The original definition provided a clear and measurable standard for tracking FTEs, while the modified definition is vague. This is used in various places, like costs of research etc. With the omission, the expenses are not well defined, and lead to a lot of problems. The term 'FTE' is still used in the contract but now lacks the necessary details for calculating costs, leading to problems in Sections that depend on FTE calculations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.39 "FTE Costs" means, with respect to a Party for any period, the applicable FTE Rate multiplied by the applicable number of FTEs of such Party performing the applicable activity described hereunder during such period.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'FTE' provided a specific, measurable standard (hours per year). The changed text removes this standard, making the definition vague. The contradicted text, 'FTE Costs' relies on the 'FTE' definition and 'FTE Rate'. Because the 'FTE' definition is now vague, the 'FTE Costs' calculation is no longer well-defined or enforceable. The omission in the changed text creates a contradiction, as the 'FTE Costs' section assumes a clearly defined 'FTE', which is now missing, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9.7 Taxes. (a) General. Each Party shall be solely responsible for the payment of all income taxes imposed on its share of income arising directly or indirectly from the activities of the Parties under this Agreement. In the event that Sanofi is required, under Applicable Law, to withhold any deduction or tax from any payment due to RevMed under this Agreement (taking into account any legally available reduction or elimination of such tax pursuant to an applicable tax treaty or otherwise), such amount will be deducted from the payment to be made by Sanofi, paid to the proper taxing authority, and Sanofi will notify RevMed and upon RevMed's request promptly provide RevMed with copies of any tax certificate or other documentation evidencing such withholding, provided, however, that in the event that any such withholding tax arises as a result of Sanofi's re-domiciliation, assignment of its rights or obligations hereunder to an Affiliate, or use of any Third Party subcontractor, payments to RevMed hereunder shall be made on a grossed-up basis to ensure that RevMed receives the same amount it would have in the absence of such withholding. Each Party agrees to cooperate with the other Party in claiming exemptions from such deductions or withholdings under any agreement or treaty from time to time in effect.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.7 Taxes. (a) General. Each Party shall be solely responsible for the payment of all income taxes imposed on its share of income arising directly or indirectly from the activities of the Parties under this Agreement. Each Party agrees to cooperate with the other Party in claiming exemptions from such deductions or withholdings under any agreement or treaty from time to time in effect.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the detailed description of withholding tax responsibilities and gross-up provisions when Sanofi changes domicile or assigns rights, this introduces a contradiction. The General provision remains that each party is reponsible for their own income tax. However the omitted section detailed how to handle witholding taxes. The contract continues to reference 'deductions or withholdings', but the mechanism for handling them is now undefined, which may lead to one party being in debt to the other. This lack of clarity can create ambiguity when the need for withholding tax arises.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.7(b) Value Added Tax. Notwithstanding anything contained in Section 9.7(a), this Section 9.7(b) will apply with respect to value added tax (or sales, use or indirect tax) ("VAT"). All payments to be made by Sanofi hereunder are exclusive of VAT. If any VAT is chargeable in respect of any such payments, Sanofi will notify RevMed and pay VAT at the applicable rate in respect of any such payments following the receipt of a VAT invoice in the appropriate form issued by RevMed in respect of those payments or Sanofi shall self-assess and pay such VAT, such VAT to be payable on the later of the due date of the payment to which such VAT relates and [***] after the receipt by Sanofi of the applicable invoice relating to that VAT payment.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the detailed description of withholding tax responsibilities in Section 9.7(a) creates a direct contradiction with Section 9.7(b). While 9.7(a) now states that each party is responsible for their own income tax, the omitted section detailed how to handle witholding taxes, a type of income tax. Section 9.7(b) refers back to 9.7(a) regarding VAT, implying that the general tax responsibilities outlined in 9.7(a) apply. However, with the specific guidelines for withholding tax removed from 9.7(a), the mechanism for handling withholding taxes is undefined. This creates ambiguity and an in-text contradiction, making it unclear how VAT, which is considered distinct from the general responsibility for income tax by the agreement's design, should be managed in relation to the general guidelines of 9.7(a).
